      "parameters": "{\"libraryId\":\"/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps\",\"query\":\"Getting started guide: repository structure, how to run sample apps, pyds bindings, pipeline building, installation prerequisites\"}",
      "serverName": "context7"
    }
  ]

  "status": "success"

Result:

```json
{
  "result": "{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"### Install Base Dependencies for DeepStream Python Bindings (Ubuntu)\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/bindings/README.md\\n\\nInstalls essential development libraries and tools required to compile DeepStream Python bindings on Ubuntu 24.04 with Python 3.12. This includes GStreamer, GLib, and build tools.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\napt install python3-gi python3-dev python3-gst-1.0 python-gi-dev git meson \\\\\\n    python3 python3-pip python3-venv cmake g++ build-essential libglib2.0-dev \\\\\\n    libglib2.0-dev-bin libgstreamer1.0-dev libtool m4 autoconf automake libgirepository-2.0-dev libcairo2-dev\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Initialize DeepStream Python App Submodules\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/bindings/README.md\\n\\nClones the DeepStream Python Apps repository and initializes its submodules, including gst-python and pybind11, which are necessary for building the bindings. Ensure the repository is cloned under the DeepStream SDK's sources directory.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\ncd /opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/sources/deepstream_python_apps/\\ngit submodule update --init\\npython3 bindings/3rdparty/git-partial-submodule/git-partial-submodule.py restore-sparse\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Setting up a Python Virtual Environment for pyds\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/FAQ.md\\n\\nGuides users on creating and activating a Python virtual environment for Python 3.12, which is required for installing and importing pip packages like pyds. This isolates project dependencies.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\nsudo apt install python3-venv\\n\\n# Create a venv for pyds\\npython3 -m venv pyds\\n# Activate the environment\\nsource ./pyds/bin/activate\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Quick Build DeepStream Python Bindings (x86)\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/bindings/README.md\\n\\nCompiles the DeepStream Python bindings for x86-ubuntu-24.04 with Python 3.12 using the PyPA build system. Sets parallel build level to utilize all available CPU cores. The output wheel is placed in the 'dist/' directory.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\ncd deepstream_python_apps/bindings\\nexport CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=$(nproc)\\npython3 -m build\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Build and Run Deepstream GStreamer Pipeline\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/notebooks/deepstream_launchpad.ipynb\\n\\nThis function initializes GStreamer, creates and configures essential pipeline elements (nvstreammux, nvinfer, nvtracker), adds them to the pipeline, and links them appropriately. It handles input source creation, inference engine configuration, and optional tracker setup. Dependencies include GStreamer libraries, sys, and configparser.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\ndef build_and_run_pipeline():\\n    # Standard initialization procedure\\n    Gst.init(None)\\n\\n    # Create gstreamer elements\\n    # Create Pipeline element that will form a connection of other elements\\n    print(\\\"Creating Pipeline \\\\n \\\")\\n    pipeline = Gst.Pipeline()\\n\\n    if not pipeline:\\n        sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to create Pipeline \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n    number_sources = len(input_file_list) # Declare number of input sources\\n    \\n    print(\\\"Creating streammux \\\\n \\\")\\n\\n    # Create nvstreammux instance to form batches from one or more sources.\\n    streammux = Gst.ElementFactory.make(\\\"nvstreammux\\\", \\\"Stream-muxer\\\")\\n    if not streammux:\\n        sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to create NvStreamMux \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n    # Set streammux properties\\n    streammux.set_property('width', 1920)\\n    streammux.set_property('height', 1080)\\n    streammux.set_property('batch-size', number_sources)\\n    streammux.set_property('batched-push-timeout', 4000000)\\n\\n    pipeline.add(streammux) # Add streammux to the pipeline\\n    \\n    # Iterate through each input source\\n    for i in range(number_sources):\\n        print(\\\"Creating source_bin \\\",i,\\\" \\\\n \\\")\\n        uri_name=input_file_list[i] # Retrieve input file URI\\n        if uri_name.find(\\\"rtsp://\\\") == 0 :\\n            is_live = True\\n        source_bin=create_source_bin(i, uri_name) # Call helper to create source bin\\n        if not source_bin:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\"Unable to create source bin \\\\n\\\")\\n        pipeline.add(source_bin) # Add source bin to pipeline\\n        padname=\\\"sink_%u\\\" %i\\n        sinkpad= streammux.request_pad_simple(padname) # Retrieve a sink pad from the streammux element\\n        if not sinkpad:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\"Unable to create sink pad bin \\\\n\\\")\\n        srcpad=source_bin.get_static_pad(\\\"src\\\") # Retrieve the source pad of the source bin\\n        if not srcpad:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\"Unable to create src pad bin \\\\n\\\")\\n        srcpad.link(sinkpad) # Link the source pad of the source bin to the sink pad of the streammux\\n        \\n        print(\\\"Creating nvinfer (PGIE) \\\\n \\\")\\n\\n    # Create nvinfer element\\n    pgie = Gst.ElementFactory.make(\\\"nvinfer\\\", \\\"primary-inference\\\")\\n    if not pgie:\\n        sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to create pgie \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n    # Set nvinfer properties\\n    pgie.set_property('config-file-path', pgie_config_file)\\n    pgie_batch_size=pgie.get_property(\\\"batch-size\\\")\\n\\n    if(pgie_batch_size != number_sources):\\n        print(\\\"WARNING: Overriding infer-config batch-size\\\",pgie_batch_size,\\\" with number of sources \\\", number_sources,\\\" \\\\n\\\")\\n        pgie.set_property(\\\"batch-size\\\",number_sources)\\n\\n\\n    # Add pgie to pipeline\\n    pipeline.add(pgie)\\n    \\n    if enable_tracker:\\n        print(\\\"Creating nvtracker\\\\n \\\")\\n\\n        # Create nvtracker element\\n        tracker = Gst.ElementFactory.make(\\\"nvtracker\\\", \\\"tracker\\\")\\n        if not tracker:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to create tracker \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n        # Parse tracker config file and set properties\\n        config = configparser.ConfigParser()\\n        config.read(tracker_config_file)\\n        config.sections()\\n\\n        for key in config['tracker']:\\n            if key == 'tracker-width' :\\n                tracker_width = config.getint('tracker', key)\\n                tracker.set_property('tracker-width', tracker_width)\\n            if key == 'tracker-height' :\\n                tracker_height = config.getint('tracker', key)\\n                tracker.set_property('tracker-height', tracker_height)\\n            if key == 'gpu-id' :\\n                tracker_gpu_id = config.getint('tracker', key)\\n                tracker.set_property('gpu_id', tracker_gpu_id)\\n            if key == 'll-lib-file' :\\n                tracker_ll_lib_file = config.get('tracker', key)\\n                tracker.set_property('ll-lib-file', tracker_ll_lib_file)\\n            if key == 'll-config-file' :\\n                tracker_ll_config_file = config.get('tracker', key)\\n                tracker.set_property('ll-config-file', tracker_ll_config_file)\\n\\n        # Add tracker to pipeline\\n        pipeline.add(tracker)\\n        \\n        print(\\\"Creating nvinfer (SGIE1) \\\\n \\\")\\n\\n    \\n        # Create nvinfer element\\n        sgie1 = Gst.ElementFactory.make(\\\"nvinfer\\\", \\\"secondary1-nvinference-engine\\\")\\n        if not sgie1:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to make sgie1 \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n        print(\\\"Creating nvinfer (SGIE2) \\\\n \\\")\\n\\n        # Create nvinfer element\\n        sgie2 = Gst.ElementFactory.make(\\\"nvinfer\\\", \\\"secondary2-nvinference-engine\\\")\\n        if not sgie2:\\n            sys.stderr.write(\\\" Unable to make sgie2 \\\\n\\\")\\n\\n        # Set config file for each SGIE\\n        sgie1.set_property('config-file-path', sgie1_config_file)\\n        sgie2.set_property('config-file-path', sgie2_config_file)\\n\\n        # Add SGIEs to pipeline\\n        pipeline.add(sgie1)\\n        pipeline.add(sgie2)\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\"}]}"
}
```

  "result": "{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"### Launch DeepStream Test Application\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/bindings/README.md\\n\\nThis command demonstrates how to launch the 'deepstream-test1' application. It requires navigating to the application's directory and executing the Python script with a path to an input H.264 video file as an argument.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\ncd apps/deepstream-test1\\npython3 deepstream_test_1.py \u003cinput .h264 file\u003e\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Run DeepStream Python Integration Tests\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/tests/integration/README.md\\n\\nThis command executes the integration tests for the DeepStream Python bindings using pytest. It assumes that all prerequisites, including the Python virtual environment, DeepStream bindings, and test dependencies, have been installed and that the current directory is the integration tests directory.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\npytest test.py\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### Setup Python 3.12 Virtual Environment for DeepStream Tests\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/tests/integration/README.md\\n\\nThis script sets up a Python 3.12 virtual environment named 'env' for running DeepStream integration tests. It first ensures the python3-venv package is installed and then creates the virtual environment in the current directory. Activating this environment is necessary before installing project dependencies.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\napt install python3-venv\\npython3 -m venv env\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n### DeepStream Python Apps \u003e Running Sample Applications\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/HOWTO.md\\n\\nClone the deepstream_python_apps repo under \u003cDeepStream ROOT\u003e/sources:\\ngit clone https://github.com/NVIDIA-AI-IOT/deepstream_python_apps\\n\\nThis will create the following directory:\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\u003cDeepStream ROOT\u003e/sources/deepstream_python_apps&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\nThe Python apps are under the \\\"apps\\\" directory. Go into each app directory and follow instructions in the README. \\n\\nNOTE: The app configuration files contain relative paths for models.\\n\\n--------------------------------\\n\\n### 3 Installing the bindings \u003e 3.2 Launching test-1 app\\n\\nSource: https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/bindings/README.md\\n\\nTo launch the &#96;deepstream-test1&#96; application, navigate to the &#96;apps/deepstream-test1&#96; directory and execute the Python script, providing the path to an input &#96;.h264&#96; file as an argument. The command would look like this: &#96;cd apps/deepstream-test1&#96; followed by &#96;python3 deepstream_test_1.py \u003cinput .h264 file\u003e&#96;.\"}]}"
}

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Here is what **Context7** returns for **`deepstream_python_apps`** (library ID: `/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps`).

## What it is

**DeepStream Python Apps** is the repo that ships **Python bindings (`pyds`)** and **sample apps** for the DeepStream SDK—GStreamer pipelines, inference, tracking, etc., from Python.

- **Repo (browse on GitHub):** [NVIDIA-AI-IOT/deepstream_python_apps](https://github.com/NVIDIA-AI-IOT/deepstream_python_apps)

## Where to read the “guide”

| Doc | Role |
|-----|------|
| **[HOWTO.md](https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/HOWTO.md)** | Clone location, `apps/` layout, note about model paths in configs |
| **[bindings/README.md](https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/bindings/README.md)** | Dependencies, submodules, **building bindings**, running **deepstream-test1** |
| **[FAQ.md](https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/FAQ.md)** | venv / Python 3.12 notes for `pyds` |

## Practical flow (from Context7 snippets)

1. **Clone** under your DeepStream tree, e.g.  
   `<DeepStream ROOT>/sources/deepstream_python_apps`  
   (see [HOWTO.md](https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/HOWTO.md)).

2. **Submodules** (for building bindings), from that folder:
   ```bash
   git submodule update --init
   python3 bindings/3rdparty/git-partial-submodule/git-partial-submodule.py restore-sparse
   ```

3. **Samples** live under **`apps/`**; each app has its own README. Example run ([bindings README](https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/bindings/README.md)):
   ```bash
   cd apps/deepstream-test1
   python3 deepstream_test_1.py <input .h264 file>
   ```

4. **Bindings build** (x86 example with `python3 -m build` from `bindings/`) and **Ubuntu deps** are documented in [bindings/README.md](https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/bindings/README.md).

5. **Integration tests:** [tests/integration/README.md](https://github.com/nvidia-ai-iot/deepstream_python_apps/blob/master/tests/integration/README.md) — venv + `pytest test.py`.

For the **full SDK** (install, concepts, C API, etc.), Context7 also lists **`/websites/nvidia_metropolis_deepstream_dev-guide`** (NVIDIA DeepStream dev guide); the Python repo above is the **Python-specific** piece.

If you tell me your DeepStream install path and whether you need **build-from-source** or **prebuilt wheel** only, I can narrow the exact steps for your machine.
